name: Kulich
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak raisins in rum for at least 30 minutes or overnight.
2. In a small bowl, combine sugar, yeast, and warm water. Let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
3. In a large bowl with mixer, beat together remaining sugar, butter, eggs, vanilla, powdered milk, salt, and saffron (if using). Add yeast mixture and 2 cups of flour, beating for 2 minutes at high speed.
4. Drain raisins and reserved rum for glaze. Stir raisins, almonds, and orange peel into dough with a wooden spoon. Mix in enough flour to form a soft dough. Turn onto a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 minutes.
5. Grease two 1 lb. coffee cans well. Line bottoms with waxed paper rounds.
6. Turn dough out onto a floured surface. Punch down; knead a few turns. Divide in half and place in prepared cans. Let rise, covered, for about 1 1/2 hours, or until it has risen to the top of the can.
7. Preheat oven to moderate (350°F). Brush tops of breads lightly with water.
8. Bake in the lower third of the preheated moderate oven (350°F) for 35-40 minutes, or until golden brown on top and a long skewer inserted in the center comes out clean. Check after 25 minutes; if browning too quickly, tent with foil.
9. Using oven mitts, carefully remove bread from cans, supporting the top of the bread and twisting off the can. Cool upright on a wire rack to room temperature. Drizzle glaze over breads. Garnish with candied orange peel and slivered almonds, if desired.
Sugar Glaze:
Stir together confectioners' sugar, reserved soaking rum, lemon juice, and water in a small bowl until it has a good drizzling consistency.
